title: Wire the visual-wrap model into the TUI input field
task:
The norn-tui input field has a half-wired visual-wrap model. wrap.rs computes width-aware visual rows (including CJK/emoji widths) and InputEditor already tracks visual_cursor_up/down, viewport_top, and scroll_to_cursor — but the renderer (app/render.rs::render_input) paints raw LOGICAL lines and the fixed-panel height (set_input_area) is set from LOGICAL line count. The result: a single line longer than the terminal width soft-wraps via the terminal's own auto-wrap, but the panel only reserves one row, so the wrapped overflow paints over the scroll region above. Long input grows unbounded too because viewport_top/scroll_to_cursor are never wired into the renderer.

OUTCOME — the visible end-user behaviour after this change:
- Typing a line that exceeds the terminal width grows the input panel downward to accommodate every wrapped visual row; the conversation above and the status bar below are NOT overwritten.
- The cursor sits at the correct visual position when a line wraps (display column relative to its visual row, not its logical line).
- Resizing the terminal narrower grows the input panel so newly-wrapped rows stay visible; resizing wider shrinks it.
- Very long input is capped at min(terminal_rows / 2, 12) visual rows; beyond that the input scrolls internally so the cursor stays visible. Up/Down/PageUp-style motion past the cap moves the viewport, not the panel size.
- The dead duplicate InputEditor::render method (test-only, replaced by app/render.rs::render_input) is removed so there is one paint path, not two.

requirements.description: Panel sizing must reflect visual (wrapped) height, not logical line count. In crates/norn-tui/src/app/edit.rs the final call `state.fixed_panel.set_input_area(state.input_editor.height())` uses logical height; switch to `state.input_editor.visual_height(cols)` (cols is already a parameter of apply_edit_action). Any other production site that calls set_input_area after an editor mutation must also use visual_height — sweep them. Do not change set_input_area's signature; the editor's visual_height(cols) already exists in input/editor.rs.
requirements.acceptance: Unit test in app/edit.rs (or a new test file under app/) that inserts one logical line of 60 characters into the editor, calls apply_edit_action with cols=20, and asserts state.fixed_panel.total_height() reflects three visual input rows (not one). All set_input_area call sites in production code paths now go through visual_height. cargo test -p norn-tui passes; cargo clippy -p norn-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

requirements.description: Rewrite render_input in crates/norn-tui/src/app/render.rs so it paints VISUAL rows from wrap::layout instead of raw logical lines. Use wrap::layout(lines, cursor_row, cursor_col, cols) to get the visual rows and the visual cursor. Iterate the layout's rows: for each VisualRow, slice the logical line (lines[row.logical_row]) by character indices [row.char_start, row.char_end) and write it at input_top + (visual_row_idx - viewport_top), erasing the line first. Park the terminal cursor at input_top + (cursor.visual_row - viewport_top), cursor.display_col. Skip visual rows outside the viewport window. This file currently writes raw \x1b[ escapes; keep that approach (consistent with the rest of app/render.rs) — do not switch to termina Csi types just for this change. Honour CO7/CO8: every write is cursor-addressed within fixed-panel territory only; no scroll-region writes.
requirements.acceptance: Unit test that constructs an editor with the single logical line 'abcdefghij', cursor at end (col=10), and renders into a buffer at width=5. Output contains 'abcde' at the first input row and 'fghij' at the second, with the cursor parked at column 5 of the second row. Existing input-editor tests still pass. Multi-line wrap test: input 'short\nverylonglineHERE' at width=10 produces three visual rows in the painted output.

requirements.description: Cap the input area at min(terminal_rows / 2, 12) visual rows. When visual_height(cols) exceeds the cap, set_input_area must be called with the cap, not the full visual height. After every edit-action that may have moved the cursor (in apply_edit_action) call state.input_editor.scroll_to_cursor(cols, cap) so viewport_top tracks the cursor. render_input must then paint only the visual rows in the window [viewport_top, viewport_top + cap), translating visual_row indices into panel-relative rows by subtracting viewport_top. The cap value belongs in a single named const (e.g. INPUT_AREA_MAX_ROWS = 12) in app/render.rs or app/edit.rs — easy to retune. terminal_rows is obtainable the same way render_input currently obtains rows; thread it into apply_edit_action if it isn't already passed.
requirements.acceptance: Unit test: 50 lines of single-character content, cursor at the last line, cols=80, terminal_rows=24 → state.fixed_panel reports input_area_rows == 12 (the cap, since 24/2 == 12). viewport_top is positioned so the cursor's visual row is within [viewport_top, viewport_top + 12). visual_cursor_up sufficient times moves the cursor upward AND scrolls viewport_top down. visual_cursor_down at the bottom of the cap pushes viewport_top forward. cargo test -p norn-tui passes.

requirements.description: Delete the dead duplicate paint method. crates/norn-tui/src/input/editor.rs defines InputEditor::render (around lines 262-299) which is only ever called from its own unit test (render_addresses_each_input_row_and_parks_the_cursor). Production paints via app/render.rs::render_input. Remove the InputEditor::render method, remove the test that exercises it, and drop any imports that become unused as a result (use crate::render::sync_render, termina Csi/Cursor/Edit/EraseInLine in editor.rs may go).
requirements.acceptance: ripgrep for 'InputEditor::render' or '.render(' against an InputEditor receiver returns zero hits across the workspace. The named test is removed. cargo build -p norn-tui succeeds with no dead-code warnings. cargo clippy -p norn-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

requirements.description: Verify the terminal-resize path recomputes the panel using visual_height. Find the resize event handler in crates/norn-tui/src/app/event_loop.rs (search for Resize / WindowChange / get_dimensions on the resize path). After a resize, set_input_area must be called with state.input_editor.visual_height(new_cols), and scroll_to_cursor(new_cols, cap) must run so the cursor stays visible at the new width. If the resize site already triggers a full redraw via the post-edit recompute path that R1 fixes, that satisfies this requirement — but verify it explicitly and add a unit test if the resize path is independent.
requirements.acceptance: Manual or unit-test demonstration: an editor with a 60-char single line at cols=80 reports panel input_area == 1; after switching to cols=20 the panel input_area == 3. If a unit test is not feasible against the real resize handler, a comment in event_loop.rs explicitly names the visual_height recompute and references R5.

context:
FILES — primary:
- crates/norn-tui/src/input/editor.rs (InputEditor data model; visual_height + viewport_top already exist; InputEditor::render is the dead duplicate to delete)
- crates/norn-tui/src/input/navigation.rs (cursor/edit mutations + scroll_to_cursor + visual_cursor_up/down — already correct, just unused by the renderer)
- crates/norn-tui/src/input/wrap.rs (the visual-row layout — already correct, handles CJK/emoji widths)
- crates/norn-tui/src/app/edit.rs (apply_edit_action — currently calls set_input_area(height()), needs visual_height(cols) + scroll_to_cursor)
- crates/norn-tui/src/app/render.rs (render_input — needs to paint visual rows from wrap::layout instead of raw logical lines; also home for the INPUT_AREA_MAX_ROWS const)
- crates/norn-tui/src/app/event_loop.rs (resize handler site for R5)
- crates/norn-tui/src/render/fixed_panel.rs (set_input_area — DO NOT change its signature; just call it with visual_height)

WHAT'S ALREADY CORRECT — wrap::layout, visual_cursor_up/down, cursor_on_first/last_visual_line, scroll_to_cursor, viewport_top, visual_height. These primitives all work; this task is wiring them into the panel-sizing and render paths.

WHAT'S ALREADY WIRED — app/edit.rs already dispatches Up/Down to visual_cursor_up/down (with history fall-through when on the first/last visual line). Keep that behaviour.

DESIGN CONSTRAINTS — DESIGN.md CO7: scroll-region writes never use cursor addressing. CO8: the fixed panel is the only cursor-addressed rendering. The existing render_input already obeys both (every write is cursor-addressed within fixed-panel rows); the visual-row rewrite must preserve this.

STYLE — crate-wide standards from /Users/tom/Developer/ablative/yggdrasil/CLAUDE.md: no unwrap/expect/panic/todo in production code (clippy denies them); pedantic clippy clean; no TODO/FIXME/HACK markers; files under 500 LOC (excluding tests/comments/whitespace); strict thiserror in library code. The existing app/render.rs uses raw \x1b[ escapes — keep that idiom in the rewrite; don't reach for termina Csi just for the new code.

EDGE CASES TO HANDLE — empty editor (one empty logical line → one visual row); cursor at exact wrap boundary (wrap::layout puts cursor at display_col 0 of the next visual row); multi-line input where only one line wraps (visual_height is the sum of per-line visual rows); CJK/emoji widths (wrap::layout already handles, just trust it); viewport at the exact cap boundary (cursor.visual_row == viewport_top + cap - 1 must remain visible — no off-by-one).

TESTS — input/editor.rs and input/navigation.rs already have ~30 unit tests for the data model; the wrap.rs tests pin visual layout. Add tests for the new render and sizing behaviour rather than touching the existing tests. Aim for: panel-size-tracks-visual-height, render_paints_visual_rows, scroll_clamps_to_cap, cursor_visible_after_scroll, resize_narrower_grows_panel.

VERIFICATION — cargo check -p norn-tui, cargo clippy -p norn-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test -p norn-tui, plus a workspace grep for TODO/FIXME/HACK/unimplemented! in changed files.
